TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_domestic_troubles_Muu

S.FORM: sua (COUGH) sua iku masin ia sua nganaraku ia Mu'u kisʉʉn Kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 現在我，我的名字在kanakanavu裡說成Mu'u。
Now I, my name is called Mu'u in Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: sua te ku poisua sua makai tanasa mia tanasa maku sii marang ci kia.
S.TRANSL: 我要說那個......像是我的家，因為我老了。
I will talk about...like my home, because I become old.

S.FORM: cucuru kan 'una kia manu nakai 'esi namanamarʉ.
S.TRANSL: 說實在的，我是有小孩，但是在平地。
To be frank, I have children, but they are in the flatland.

S.FORM: ka'an er ka'an tavarʉ'ʉ movua mamarang pirati'ing sii 'una kavangvang tating.
S.TRANSL: 沒辦法給長輩一點錢，因為都有小孩子了。
(They) cannot give the elderly a little money, because (they) all have children.

S.FORM: 'una neci'ire isua ikim masin mata er miciku ia marang ci kim.
S.TRANSL: 你看，我現在跟Miciku，我們老了。
See, now I and Miciku get old.

S.FORM: tassa marang ci kimi cucuru 'ecikia mamia kan aka paira maa cʉpʉnga ku si te ci kiae kusa makenanu?.
S.TRANSL: 我們兩個都老了，我經常一直很難過。我究竟該怎麼辦呢？
Both of us get old, and I keep being upset often. So what should I do (with that)?

S.FORM: te ci kia tʉvʉtʉvʉtʉvʉsʉ mamamane.
necire kusa tia cumacʉ'ʉra mamanu misaa ku mamia pacʉpʉcʉpʉng.
S.TRANSL: 我一直在想，究竟誰要看顧我的小孩。
I keep thinking about who on earth is going to take care of my child.

S.FORM: apaco'e nuu mona ku in na 'umo'uma ia makasua cʉpʉnga ku.
S.TRANSL: 縱然我是在田裡，我所想的就是那個樣子。
Although I am in the field, what I think about is that way.

S.FORM: caren kia kʉcʉ sii makasia 'akia cu mamarang.
S.TRANSL: 我很可憐，(因此我得忍耐)，因為我父母不在了。
I am poor and therefore (I should bear it), because my parents are already not (alive).

S.FORM: koo ci kia cʉmʉra cumaku koo ci kia cʉmʉra cinaa maku 'ecikia mamia parʉrʉ'ʉ a.
S.TRANSL: 我沒有看到爸爸，我沒有看到媽媽，我只有懷念的份。
I don't see my father, I don't see my mother, I can just only miss (them).

S.FORM: nimike kia kʉcʉ nanu mia a'ai pi kia misa kia pacʉpʉcʉpʉng.
S.TRANSL: 我一直那樣想，我從嬰兒開始，是如何（走過這一生的）。
I keep think in this way, how (I have spent my life) since babyhood.

S.FORM: imo makasin ia ka'an ci kia tavarʉ'ʉ kavavantuku marang ci kia.
S.TRANSL: 所以我現在不能賺錢，我老了。
Therefore now I can't make money; I am old.

S.FORM: ka'an ci kia tavarʉ'ʉ kanupanupasʉ marang ci kia.
S.TRANSL: 我不能賺錢；我老了。
I can't make money; I am old.

S.FORM: neci'ire er sua Kanakanavu makasi masin ia er ma icu ku mamia marang ta marang kisʉʉn.
S.TRANSL: 你看，Kanakanavu現在只有我是所謂的最年長的。
You see,in Kanakanavu now only I am the so-called the eldest.

S.FORM: 'esi 'una ken tia sikamanʉng Kanakanavu kaisisi avara'e mamia ia manasʉ pokari pa 'ikua.
S.TRANSL: 只要是任何關於Kanakanavu的祭典，一定還要邀請我。
As long as there are any Kanakanavu ceremonies, (they) are sure to invite me.

S.FORM: er 'inanu ikasu cuma Mu'u?.
S.TRANSL: Mu'u叔叔，你在哪裡？
Uncle Mu'u, where are you?

S.FORM: er ivata sian ikasu tia tapaipecaa kari sii.
S.TRANSL: 你來這裡，因為要透過你說話（和族人或是祖先）。
You come here because (we) want talk through you (with the clansmen or ancestors).

S.FORM: nakai isi ia mamanʉng sii iku nuu 'apatanganai ku tamu kakangca tamvina'ʉ misee ia macangcangarʉ cʉpʉnga ku sii necʉ'ʉre.
S.TRANSL: 但是這是很好的，因為如果透過我來跟上蒼和小米神說話，我的心裡很高興。
But this is good, because if (our people) talk with the god and the god of millet through me, I will be happy in my mind.

S.FORM: makai kisaa mamarang miana mate'e kitana manu mamanu.
S.TRANSL: 就像以前老人家所說的，（部落的傳統）留給我們這些後代。
Like what the old people said in the past, (the tribal traditions) were passed down to us children.

S.FORM: ikam maman ia 'akun aka cʉpʉng.
S.TRANSL: 你們後代阿，心裡不要難過。
You offspring, do not worry.

S.FORM: caniara noo kumaree kam vina'ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 如果你們種植小米...
If you plant the millet...

S.FORM: mak macʉpʉcʉpʉng cu mamia manasʉ te cu kam mon na cakʉrʉ kaisisi.
S.TRANSL: 就只要去想(了)...你們或許就要在聚會所祭祀了。
Only remember…You may be about to worship ancestors in the chamber.

S.FORM: mikoo kisaa mamarang sii 'e cu tia marivina'ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 老人家所說的米貢，因為已經要收割小米了。
What the elder call ‘MIKOON’, because we are already going to harvest the millet.

S.FORM: ka'an pa aka cʉpʉng sua isua.
S.TRANSL: 那個心裡還不會難過。
That, will not be sad yet.

S.FORM: imoo misaa ku mamia sua ikita kanakanavu ia sua katatamu misee ia ’akun sua masi’upupang.
S.TRANSL: 所以我只有說，我們卡那卡那富人呢，所謂的祭酒呢，不可以亂說(祭詞)。
So I just say, we Kanakanavu people, the so-called libation, (one) cannot say (prayers) casually.

S.FORM: sua ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ pokarikari ia ’akun mamia pokarikari.
S.TRANSL: 不會說(祭詞)(的人)，就不要說話。
Those who don’t know how to say, don’t speak then.

S.FORM: tia kari ucanen maamia sua kari musu.
S.TRANSL: 你要說的(祭詞)只有一句。
What you will say is only one sentence.

S.FORM: tamoo aman cu kasu mamarang misee mamia ’akun sua ’una kari noo katatamu kita noo ’una kiten kari aka ia sii sua tavara’a mamia.
S.TRANSL: 祖先阿，我們祭酒的時候，我們若有不好的話，因為(大家)要謹記。
Our ancestors! When we libate, if we have bad prayers.

S.FORM: necʉre.
S.TRANSL: 你看!
Look!

S.FORM: AH makanan tia s te kita cumacʉ’ʉra maman mita sikaisisi maman mita makan matʉa maka mataa sikaisisi ta mamarang.
S.TRANSL: 要怎樣(祭祀)呢，我們要看我們後代祭祀的方式和老一輩的人究竟是否一樣。
How to worship ancestors ? We will see if the way our offspring worship is the same as the way the elderly did.

S.FORM: misee AH mamarang mita.
S.TRANSL: 我們祖先會說，和我們祖先祭祀的方式(包括祭詞/動作)相同嗎?
Our ancestors will say that, is it the same as the way our ancestors worshiped?

S.FORM: imoo ku misee sua mokusa kita cakʉrʉ ia ’akun masipupang.
S.TRANSL: 所以我說，我們去聚會所呢，不要亂說話。
So I say, when we go to the chamber, (we) do not speak casually.

S.FORM: sua ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ pokarikari ia tamoo kisaa mamia.
S.TRANSL: 不會說(祭詞)的(人)，只要說「tamoo」。
Those who don’t know how to say just say ‘tamoo’.

S.FORM: nguaci sua kari hua.
S.TRANSL: 只有那句(講完就結束的意思)。
Only that word.

S.FORM: necʉre kan ’una kita ’e na Kanakanavu (IHI) pʉ’ʉpʉ kanakanavu i masiupupang noo katatamu kita cakʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 你看，我們有在…我們這些遺留下來的knv(人) ，當我們在為聚會所祭酒的時候，隨便說話…
You see, we have in… we the remaining Kanakanavu people, when we libate in the chamber, (we) say the prayers casually…

S.FORM: necʉ’ʉre ’una kita araaka.
S.TRANSL: 你看，我們有(人)去世。
You see, we have some people who passed away.

S.FORM: m aʉ ’aka sua tina’anin.
S.TRANSL: (有人)他的身體不好。
Some are in bad physical condition.

S.FORM: ka’an mamanʉng si’icʉp in.
S.TRANSL: 他睡不好(隱喻為他運氣不好)。
He does not sleep well. (MET. He has bad luck)

S.FORM: sipʉcʉcʉp in sua makasua sua.
S.TRANSL: 他的運氣就像那樣啊。
His (bad) luck is like that.

S.FORM: oh ka’an masimanʉngʉn mon na cakʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 在聚會所沒有好好(正確地)地說(祭詞)。
The prayers are not said in the right way in the chamber.

S.FORM: noo kaisisi kita ia matacuvucuvung kita ia manasʉ ku paira pu’una kari kamua.
S.TRANSL: 當我們祭祀的時候(之前)，我們召集族人聚在一起，我常一定有話先跟你們說。
When we worship, we gather together and I often will something to tell you first.

S.FORM: aah mamanu ’akunen masipupang katatamu kamu.
S.TRANSL: 哎呀，孩子們，你們祭酒不要隨便(亂)說(祭詞)。
Ah, children! Don’t say the prayers casually when you libate.

S.FORM: masimanʉnga sua ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ pokarikari ia ’akun maa pokarikari misaa ku.
S.TRANSL: 我(就常)說，不會說(祭詞)的就不要說話。
Those who don’t know how to say don’t speak then, I say.

S.FORM: mataa ucani ia.
S.TRANSL: (另外有)一件。
There’s another thing.

S.FORM: caniava noo makai mikong kita kaisisi ia cucuru makai uh… ak sua ikita mamisa ia ’esi ci kita mamia umara mamia nganee seehu seehu misee kita mamia.
S.TRANSL: 若是像米貢祭，我們(舉行)米貢祭(的時候)，我們只是說，我們最近(近年來)只是在拿政府的名稱(名義)(來辦祭典)而已。
Like Mikong, when we have the Mikong Festival, we only say that recently we celebrate it merely for the sake of the government.

S.FORM: cucuru sua mamarang mita miana ia ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ ’apacʉ’ʉra pakisia noo kaisisi.
S.TRANSL: 我們的老人家祭祀的時候真的不可以讓講閩南話的人看(米貢祭)。
Our ancestors indeed would not let Taiwanese-speaking people see (Mikong) when worshiping.

S.FORM: ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ ’apivatu na tapininga cakʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 絕對不讓來到聚會所的廣場。

S.FORM: uh matarava mamia noo nikaisisi ci kita makai vina’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 只有到我們已經祭祀過了的時候，像小米的祭祀。
Only when we finish worshiping, like millet.

S.FORM: tavarʉ’ʉn makasua pakisia.
S.TRANSL: 我們可以這樣對待閩南人。
We can treat the Chinese-speaking people that way.

S.FORM: imo ikita Kanakanavu kan sikaisisi mita ia aka makaa maraa aka sii.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們卡那卡那富祭祀的方式比較不好，因為…
So the way we worship is not good, is worse, because…

S.FORM: iavutu cu ma kitana pakisia uh aratʉm aratʉm aratʉm mamia.
S.TRANSL: 平地人直接到我們這裡(祭祀的場所)，只是突然間冒出來冒出來。
The Chinese people came straight to our place, and they just showed up.

S.FORM: ka’an ci kita tavarʉ’ʉ kaisisi.
S.TRANSL: 我們就無法進行儀式了。
We couldn’t proceed our worshiping.

S.FORM: isua sua kan hayab m maka hayab.
S.TRANSL: 那就是…比較會…
That is…more…

S.FORM: makanan kusai secu’ura kitana tamu kakangca tamvina’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 究竟我們在天上的祖先還有小米的祖先會怎樣看待我們。
How exactly would our ancestors and the god of millet see us?

S.FORM: sii makasua kita ’esi pi kita mamia tia matacuvucuvung makarikari.
S.TRANSL: 因為我們像那樣，我們還只是在一起討論當中。
Because we are like that, we’re still gathering together, discussing.

S.FORM: ’esi ci nia pakisia kuratumuru mamia.
S.TRANSL: 平地人就已經在那裏佔得滿滿的。
The Chinese people were just everywhere.

S.FORM: te kita manan mangarigari.
S.TRANSL: 我們要怎樣活動?
How do we proceed?

S.FORM: sii sua taniara soni ia ’esi ku sian.
S.TRANSL: 因為今天是…我在這裡。
Because today… I’m here.

S.FORM: ’esi ku pokarikari.
S.TRANSL: 我在說話。
I’m speaking.

S.FORM: po’ʉ’ʉna po’isua sua uh tanasa maku sii.
S.TRANSL: 說故事…說那個…我的家，因為…
Tell stories about the… my home, because…

S.FORM: kan ci kia tavarʉ’ʉ kanupanupasʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我已經不能賺錢。
I’m already unable to earn money.

S.FORM: akia cu kʉna kamʉcʉ.
S.TRANSL: 連食物都沒有。
Let alone food.

S.FORM: vʉra.
S.TRANSL: 連米也沒有。
(No) rice.

S.FORM: kani ’e ci kia mamia purusʉsʉ’ʉ mamia taniara.
S.TRANSL: 我每天只有唉聲嘆氣。
I simply sigh every day.

S.FORM: paira aaka cʉpʉng aku sii iki mata miciku ia marang ci kimi.
S.TRANSL: 我常常很難過因為我和Miciku都老了。
I often feel sad because I and Miciku have become old.

S.FORM: ka’an ci kia tavarʉ putukikio.
S.TRANSL: 我們已經不能工作了。
We’re already unable to work.

S.FORM: ’una kim manu misaa kam cau nakai.
S.TRANSL: 你們說我們是有小孩子的人，但是…
You say that I’m one who has a child, but…

S.FORM: sua manu maku ia uh… takacicin kangvang ’esi namanamarʉ putikikio namanamarʉ.
S.TRANSL: 但是我的小孩都在平地，在平地工作。
But my children are all working in the city.

S.FORM: te kam makasua sua sia situturua maku kamua.
S.TRANSL: 我用以告訴你們的是那樣。
I use that to tell you.

S.FORM: noo tumana kamu kari iisi ia sua kanang ia kan mana tia paiee.
S.TRANSL: 如果你們聽到這些話，聽得懂的人或許會後悔。
If you hear these words, those who understand will probably regret.

S.FORM: ah cucuruana Cuma Mu’u ia kari Cuma Mu’u ia ’esi pomanʉngʉ.
S.TRANSL: Mu’u叔叔說的話真的是...說得很明白。
Uncle Mu’u’s words are really clear.

S.FORM: caren curu sii marang cu.
S.TRANSL: 真的很可憐，因為年長了。
It’s really pathetic, because I’m old.

S.FORM: kan ci kia tavarʉu kanupanupasʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我已經沒辦法賺錢了。
I’m already unable to make money.

S.FORM: kan ci kia tavarʉ’ʉ kipapa cau kanupanupasʉ.
S.TRANSL: 我已經不能跟著別人去賺錢。
I’m already unable to follow people and make money.

S.FORM: ’esi ci kia mamia tinpuru mamia parʉrʉ’ʉ mamia.
S.TRANSL: 我們只能坐著，只能懷念。
We just sit here, and recollect (the past).

S.FORM: te ci kiei mosa makananu.
S.TRANSL: 我要去哪裡…
Where do I go…

S.FORM: ka’an ci kia sipisipi taniara ia te ci kiei mosa makananu manman maku.
S.TRANSL: 我的日子不會SIPISIPI，究竟我的孩子怎麼辦?
My days are not SIPISIPI, then what exactly should our children do?

S.FORM: pacʉpʉngcʉpʉng misaa ku mamia pacʉpʉngcʉpʉng sua kari maku tumaturu kamua ia manasʉ matarava mamia nesi.
S.TRANSL: 我心裡就想說，我所告訴你們的話或許就到此結束。
I think, what I want to tell you ends here.

S.FORM: pacʉpʉngcʉpʉngo makas(ua) sua na va sua kari in.
S.TRANSL: 要去想，他的話原來是那樣。
Remember that, his words are like that.
